summary: Iteration 3 (Kiosk + Pharmacy) tested end-to-end. Backend: 21/21 pytest tests PASS (kiosk lookup/checkin/pay, pharmacy queue/dispense, AppointmentUpdate enum, pharmacist seed, regression). Frontend: full kiosk flow works (Landingâ†’/kioskâ†’identifyâ†’walk-in checkinâ†’QUEUE chitâ†’Payâ†’Receipt+Medicine chit). Pharmacy dashboard, all 4 demo logins, doctor/patient/reception dashboards load cleanly after fixing two missing-import bugs in AppShell.jsx that blocked every authenticated route.

minor.issue: Returned `appointment` object is STALE â€” it reflects pre-update state (status='checked_in', payment_status='unpaid') even though the DB row is correctly updated to status='ready_for_pharmacy'/payment_status='paid'. Cause: handler reads appt dict before the update_one() call and returns the same in-memory dict without re-fetching or mutating it. Fix: re-fetch with `await db.appointments.find_one({'id': appt['id']})` after the update before returning, OR update the local dict in place.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED by testing agent â€” missing imports `useQueueSocket` and `Broadcast` icon. Threw `ReferenceError: useQueueSocket is not defined`, crashing every authenticated route (pharmacy, doctor, reception, patient dashboards). Patient->Kiosk public flow was unaffected since /kiosk doesn't use AppShell.
Status string 'ready_for_pharmacy' is rendered as 'ready for_pharmacy' (only first underscore replaced). Likely using `.replace('_', ' ')` instead of `.replaceAll('_', ' ')` or `/_/g`. Cosmetic only.
Console warnings: DialogContent missing DialogTitle and Description (a11y). Doesn't break tests but should be addressed for screen-reader users (wrap title in VisuallyHidden).

action_items: Backend (minor): re-fetch or mutate the local appointment dict after the update in POST /api/kiosk/pay so the response payload reflects the new status/payment_status (the frontend in Kiosk.jsx may rely on it).
Frontend (minor): change `.replace('_', ' ')` â†’ `.replaceAll('_', ' ')` for status badges so 'ready_for_pharmacy' renders 'ready for pharmacy'.
Frontend (minor): add DialogTitle/Description (or VisuallyHidden) inside PrintChit DialogContent to silence a11y warnings.
No further critical work needed for iteration 3 â€” both demo flows (kiosk and pharmacy) work end-to-end with the missing-import fixes applied.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/components/AppShell.jsx had two unimported identifiers (useQueueSocket, Broadcast). This indicates the file was edited but not lint-checked before commit. Recommend running `yarn lint` (eslint no-undef) as a pre-commit gate.
/app/backend/server.py is now 1349 lines â€” exceeds the 700-line guideline. Suggest splitting into routers/modules (auth, appointments, kiosk, pharmacy, ai, records, ws).

POST /api/kiosk/pay computes the response payload without re-reading the just-updated document, leading to a stale `appointment` object. Pattern repeats anywhere `update_one` is followed by `clean(dict(appt))`.
Kiosk public endpoints are intentionally unauthenticated but accept any IC string â€” consider lightweight rate-limiting per IC/IP for production to mitigate enumeration.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/components/AppShell.jsx (added missing imports for useQueueSocket and Broadcast)
/app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py (NEW test file, 21 tests covering all iteration-3 endpoints + regression)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows after AppShell fix

test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md as-is. Pharmacy demo: pharmacy@medilink.io / Pharm@123 confirmed working. Other demos unchanged.
seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed called from module-scope fixture. Test-created records prefixed TEST_ (TEST_walkin, TEST_Iter3, TEST_enum). Backend tests dispense the paid appointment, so re-running the suite is idempotent (idempotency test passes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `cd /app && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep ^REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iteration3.py -v` for regression. The kiosk flow on /kiosk uses IC-880421-14-5567 and is public (no auth). Pharmacy dashboard requires login as pharmacy@medilink.io / Pharm@123 (role=pharmacist). Be aware that AppShell.jsx had missing imports â€” if the main agent rewrites that file, double-check `useQueueSocket` and `Broadcast` imports are preserved.
rca of the issue: Critical bug RCA â€” Empty pharmacy/doctor/reception/patient dashboards: AppShell.jsx referenced `useQueueSocket` (line 31) and `Broadcast` (line 85) without importing them. React threw `ReferenceError` at component-mount time, which the route boundary caught and rendered nothing for the dashboard layout. Public /kiosk route was unaffected because it does not wrap children in AppShell. Reproduction: log in as any role; dashboard area stays blank and console shows `ReferenceError: useQueueSocket is not defined`. Mitigation applied: added `import useQueueSocket from "@/hooks/useQueueSocket"` and added `Broadcast` to the existing `@phosphor-icons/react` import block. Both dashboards now render and live-sync events trigger the green Live indicator. Recommend adding eslint `no-undef` enforcement to prevent regressions.
